整套首页
unit_03 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_03

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 361 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Text Completion
Invention was (i)______ the work of the ancient Greek historians, whose writings were filled with long and often purely fictitious speeches by great historical figures. The animating force in historical writing was rhetoric rather than (ii)______. Even well into the eighteenth century, not a few historians continued to understand themselves as artists, given a license to invent.Invention was (i)______ the work of the ancient Greek historians, whose writings were filled with long and often purely fictitious speeches by great historical figures. The animating force in historical writing was rhetoric rather than (ii)______. Even well into the eighteenth century, not a few historians continued to understand themselves as artists, given a license to invent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　a hallmark of; evidence
fictitiousanimaterather
fictitious: standard unit headword | animate: standard unit headword | rather: standard unit headword
#2 TC TRIPLE
Oral traditions are (i) __________ nearly everywhere, and in nonliterate societies they do not (ii) __________ at their first exposure to the written word. Despite Goody's contention that a kind of literacy line cuts through history, dividing oral from print cultures, it seems that traditional tale-telling can (iii) __________ long after a print culture is established.Oral traditions are (i) __________ nearly everywhere, and in nonliterate societies they do not (ii) __________ at their first exposure to the written word. Despite Goody's contention that a kind of literacy line cuts through history, dividing oral from print cultures, it seems that traditional tale-telling can (iii) __________ long after a print culture is established.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　tenacious; collapse; flourish
languishtenaciousdivide
languish: standard unit headword | tenacious: standard unit headword | divide: standard unit headword
#3 Text Completion
Mr. Stevens found that home schooling, far from representing (i)______ philosophy, (ii)______ some of the most widely accepted education ideas that children should be treated as individuals, taught in small numbers, and even given a measure of discretion over their own learning.Mr. Stevens found that home schooling, far from representing (i)______ philosophy, (ii)______ some of the most widely accepted education ideas that children should be treated as individuals, taught in small numbers, and even given a measure of discretion over their own learning.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　an anomalous; embodies
philosophyrepresentanticipate
philosophy: standard unit headword | represent: standard unit headword | anticipate: standard unit headword
#4 Sentence Equivalence
British critics covering African American musicians performing in London in the 1910s had little idea how to distinguish what was authentic African American music from what was ______, but they knew such a distinction existed.British critics covering African American musicians performing in London in the 1910s had little idea how to distinguish what was authentic African American music from what was ______, but they knew such a distinction existed.
答案：D, F　spurious; specious
authenticcover
authentic: standard unit headword | cover: standard unit headword
#5 TC SINGLE
Other company insiders have recently offered testimony that __________ several of the more lurid anecdotes found in Belmer's account, though that testimony hardly minimizes the company's culture of malfeasance and mismanagement.Other company insiders have recently offered testimony that __________ several of the more lurid anecdotes found in Belmer's account, though that testimony hardly minimizes the company's culture of malfeasance and mismanagement.
答案：A　tempers
testimonyaccount
testimony: standard unit headword | account: standard unit headword

(ii)______ by the (iii)______ of the volunteer, the Galaxy Zoo team was inspired to pursue lines of research they had never even imagined.Galaxy Zoo set a standard for citizen-scientist participation project. Zealous volunteers (i)______ the project’s organizers by classifying an entire catalog of galaxies years ahead of schedule. (ii)______ by the (iii)______ of the volunteer, the Galaxy Zoo team was inspired to pursue lines of research they had never even imagined.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　astonished; Buoyed; alacrity
buoyzealousbaffle
buoy: standard unit headword | zealous: standard unit headword | baffle: standard unit headword
#7 TC DOUBLE
Wald's decision to tell Rosetta Tharpe's story to a wider audience in Shout, Sister, Shout! (i) __________ the rigor of the book's scholarship or the acuity of its theory. The scholarly quality of the work is (ii) __________ on every page, and yet it is not a dry, specialist academic tome.Wald's decision to tell Rosetta Tharpe's story to a wider audience in Shout, Sister, Shout! (i) __________ the rigor of the book's scholarship or the acuity of its theory. The scholarly quality of the work is (ii) __________ on every page, and yet it is not a dry, specialist academic tome.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　does not compromise; evident
reflectrigor
reflect: standard unit headword | rigor: standard unit headword
#8 Sentence Equivalence
As a way of ______ the negative impacts of overdependence on a single export product—crude oil—the Nigerian government passed legislation in 1999 intended to revitalize the moribund solid minerals sector.As a way of ______ the negative impacts of overdependence on a single export product—crude oil—the Nigerian government passed legislation in 1999 intended to revitalize the moribund solid minerals sector.
答案：D, E　checking; stemming
cruderevitalize

If telecommuting ever did reduce traffic congestion noticeably , the excess capacity on the highways would almost certainly be quickly (i) ___________ by changes in current travel patterns. For example, more people might decide to drive alone instead of using public transportation. Historically, transportation and communications have been (ii) __________ to each other--both of them increasing concurrently--rather than (iii) ___________ for each other.If telecommuting ever did reduce traffic congestion noticeably , the excess capacity on the highways would almost certainly be quickly (i) ___________ by changes in current travel patterns. For example, more people might decide to drive alone instead of using public transportation. Historically, transportation and communications have been (ii) __________ to each other--both of them increasing concurrently--rather than (iii) ___________ for each other.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　absorbed; spurs; substitutes
augmentsubstituterather
augment: standard unit headword | substitute: standard unit headword | rather: standard unit headword
#10 Sentence Equivalence
Laughter, like speech, is primarily a human faculty, although both functions may also exist in a more ______ form in lesser primates.Laughter, like speech, is primarily a human faculty, although both functions may also exist in a more ______ form in lesser primates.
答案：C, F　primitive; rudimentary
lesserindispensable
lesser: standard unit headword | indispensable: standard unit headword
#11 Text Completion
In adolescence, (i)______ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)______ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)______ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.In adolescence, (i)______ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)______ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)______ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　peer; cliquish social behavior; clustering
shore upbound
shore up: standard unit headword | bound: standard unit headword

Blank 2
Blank 3

(i)______ was, in a way, his very theme. Was ever a bookish soul so cracklingly unmediated, so (ii)______ raw life? The detours taken in the course of the essay, which could easily have been __________, instead proved to be a virtue because they epitomized the instability of a period characterized by constant experimentation and change.The detours taken in the course of the essay, which could easily have been __________, instead proved to be a virtue because they epitomized the instability of a period characterized by constant experimentation and change.

Blank 3

While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such ______ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such ______ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.
答案：A, B　a generic; an inclusive
accent
accent: standard unit headword

Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey’s book is representative of a heightened sense of (i)_______. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii)_______ the (iii)_______ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey’s book is representative of a heightened sense of (i)_______. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii)_______ the (iii)_______ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　candor; antidote to; banal notion
cronyism
cronyism: standard unit headword

答案：E, F　simplicity; intelligibility

Blank 2
Blank 3

They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. Blank 2
Blank 3

virtuosity: standard unit headword

Nineteenth-century England presents something of (i) __________ , at least to those who believe that (ii) ___________ best promotes business ethics. The defining characteristic of English company law at that time was its extreme degree of permissiveness: laissez-faire ruled in Parliament, caveat emptor in the courts, creating the ideal breeding ground for fraud and dishonesty. Yet these were the years when London became the financial center of the world and established its status as a (iii) ____________ place to do business.Nineteenth-century England presents something of (i) __________ , at least to those who believe that (ii) ___________ best promotes business ethics. The defining characteristic of English company law at that time was its extreme degree of permissiveness: laissez-faire ruled in Parliament, caveat emptor in the courts, creating the ideal breeding ground for fraud and dishonesty. Yet these were the years when London became the financial center of the world and established its status as a (iii) ____________ place to do business.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　an enigma; regulation; comparatively safe
individualism

答案：B, F　inaccessible; opaque
distressing

答案：B, F　turbid; roiled

答案：D, F　an extremist; a zealot

History is about recognizing continuities and therefore the historian must (i) __________ stories that rope together the disparate worlds of past and present. But it is also true that honest history arises from a sense of (ii) __________. It is the experience of discontinuity that alerts us to the (iii) __________ of the past and sets us looking for narratives to capture that unfamiliar entity.History is about recognizing continuities and therefore the historian must (i) __________ stories that rope together the disparate worlds of past and present. But it is also true that honest history arises from a sense of (ii) __________. It is the experience of discontinuity that alerts us to the (iii) __________ of the past and sets us looking for narratives to capture that unfamiliar entity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　weave; interruptedness; strangeness
shun
shun: standard unit headword
#48 SE
The artist reworked certain of his etchings in order to make the scenes they depict more logical, yet, paradoxically, the pictorial space seems to become more __________ the more realistically it is defined.The artist reworked certain of his etchings in order to make the scenes they depict more logical, yet, paradoxically, the pictorial space seems to become more __________ the more realistically it is defined.
答案：D, F　fanciful; chimerical

答案：B, F　indifference; apathy

Mark Review Help 0 1111 8 Back ' Next + .. Section 5 of 51 Question 4 ol 15 If telecommuting ever did reduce traffic congestion noticeably, the excess capacity on the highways would almost certainly be quickly (i) _ _ by changes in current travel patterns. For example, more people might decide to drive alone instead of using public transportation. Historically, transportation and communications have been (ii) _ _ to each other-both of them increasing concurrently-rather than (iii) _ _ for each other.Mark Review Help 0 1111 8 Back ' Next + .. Section 5 of 51 Question 4 ol 15 If telecommuting ever did reduce traffic congestion noticeably, the excess capacity on the highways would almost certainly be quickly (i) _ _ by changes in current travel patterns. For example, more people might decide to drive alone instead of using public transportation. Historically, transportation and communications have been (ii) _ _ to each other-both of them increasing concurrently-rather than (iii) _ _ for each other.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　absorbed; spurs; substitutes
augmentsubstituterather
augment: standard unit headword | substitute: standard unit headword | rather: standard unit headword
#100 Sentence Equivalence
As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her ______; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her ______; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.
答案：B, D　humility; modesty
ratherrepresent
rather: standard unit headword | represent: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　liminal; intermediate
rather

答案：C　novelty

In other words, Venus may "freak out" occasionally, getting rid of its internal heat in great planetwide (iii) __________ of activity, rather than in the steadier cycling of lithospheric plates that occurs on Earth.Models of the interior evolution of Venus suggest that the planet (i) __________ periods of relative (ii) __________ and periods of instability and rapid surface overturn. In other words, Venus may "freak out" occasionally, getting rid of its internal heat in great planetwide (iii) __________ of activity, rather than in the steadier cycling of lithospheric plates that occurs on Earth.

Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　oscillates between; quiescence; paroxysms

In fact, this view of Boyle (ii)_______ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle’s corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i)_______ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. Blank 2

The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity

#152 Text Completion
Rocks of the Morrison Formation in the western United States yield many dinosaur fossils but few plant fossils. This apparent (i)______ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii)______ , noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.Rocks of the Morrison Formation in the western United States yield many dinosaur fossils but few plant fossils. This apparent (i)______ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii)______ , noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　dearth; demurred
dearth
dearth: standard unit headword

The prioritization by academic institutions of scholarly publication as a criterion for advancement in science departments discourages researchers from sharing their data with peers. As a result, scientists operate in a realm where the incentive structure serves as an(i) greater (ii) in the profession.The prioritization by academic institutions of scholarly publication as a criterion for advancement in science departments discourages researchers from sharing their data with peers. As a result, scientists operate in a realm where the incentive structure serves as an(i) greater (ii) in the profession.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　impediment to; collaboration
rigor

Smith’s poetry that (i)_________ its formal and aesthetic qualities are (ii)________, as Smith’s formal choices are ultimately (iii)_______ her political arguments.Analyses of Tracy K. Smith’s poetry that (i)_________ its formal and aesthetic qualities are (ii)________, as Smith’s formal choices are ultimately (iii)_______ her political arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　prioritize; deficient; ancillary to
commend
commend: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　diffuse; spread

答案：E, F　dearth; paucity

Blank 1
Blank 2

#158 TC TRIPLE
The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　More than; affirmative; recovery
hamper
hamper: standard unit headword

Sometimes people (ii)______ their errors; those same reviewers today, comparing the predictions with actual events, acknowledge how (iii)______ the author was.

Blank 3

Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. #174 Text Completion
Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare, even movement from poor to middle class was (i)______. Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, “yielded rather (ii)______ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America.” So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)______ if only the public schools did a better job.Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare, even movement from poor to middle class was (i)______. Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, “yielded rather (ii)______ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America.” So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)______ if only the public schools did a better job.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　an anomaly; pessimistic; obliterated
rather
rather: standard unit headword

答案：A, F　consequence; result

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　secondhand testimony; anecdote
testimony

#177 TC TRIPLE
Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　shallow; indifferent; poverty
authenticrigor
authentic: standard unit headword | rigor: standard unit headword

One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely ______ fossils throughout his career.One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely ______ fossils throughout his career.
答案：A, F　counterfeit; spurious
hoax
hoax: standard unit headword

The capacity for rational thought and its manifestation in language have long been assumed to distinguish human from nonhuman animals, but apes' proven ability to learn and teach sign language to other apes suggests that such a strict human-animal divide is __________.The capacity for rational thought and its manifestation in language have long been assumed to distinguish human from nonhuman animals, but apes' proven ability to learn and teach sign language to other apes suggests that such a strict human-animal divide is __________.
答案：B, C　indefensible; untenable
untenabledivide

#180 TC TRIPLE
Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious

Studies of hermaphroditic plants may exhibit sampling bias against self-fertilizing and cross-fertilizing species, thus inflating the frequency of species using a mixed mating system (both self-fertilizing and cross-fertilizing); nevertheless, the number of mixed-system species is not ______.Studies of hermaphroditic plants may exhibit sampling bias against self-fertilizing and cross-fertilizing species, thus inflating the frequency of species using a mixed mating system (both self-fertilizing and cross-fertilizing); nevertheless, the number of mixed-system species is not ______.

Blank 2

Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)______ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)______ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)______ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)______ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：B, E　dismissive of; scornful of

#205 Text Completion
People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　gloat; circumspect; candid in
testimony
testimony: standard unit headword
#206 Sentence Equivalence
Advocates for workers’ right have adopted a new strategy, one that will require considerable ingenuity but that, if successful, could ______ a movement aimed at making labor rights an unassailable feature of American democracy.Advocates for workers’ right have adopted a new strategy, one that will require considerable ingenuity but that, if successful, could ______ a movement aimed at making labor rights an unassailable feature of American democracy.
答案：B, E　galvanize; animate
animate
animate: standard unit headword
#207 SE
We might be much closer to building the next generation of observatories if competing groups of astronomers had not repeated chances to collaborate; instead, three separate projects with common goals are fighting one another for funding.We might be much closer to building the next generation of observatories if competing groups of astronomers had not repeated chances to collaborate; instead, three separate projects with common goals are fighting one another for funding.
答案：A, D　spurned; rebuffed
sabotage
sabotage: standard unit headword
#208 Text Completion
Parker’s model of human affairs reflects (i)______ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally (ii)______ premises that her colleagues in the economics department adopt in their work. Accordingly, her conclusions (iii)______ theirs.Parker’s model of human affairs reflects (i)______ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally (ii)______ premises that her colleagues in the economics department adopt in their work. Accordingly, her conclusions (iii)______ theirs.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　a sanguine; pessimistic; diverge markedly from

reflect: standard unit headword
#209 Sentence Equivalence

答案：B, F　a hierarchical; a stratified
stratify
stratify: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　supplant; replace
augment
augment: standard unit headword
#211 Text Completion
To the avid reader of E. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii)______ and ambition.To the avid reader of E. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii)______ and ambition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predictable; purview
represent
represent: standard unit headword

答案：A, F　hamper; impede

答案：B, F　supplant; replace

But it is harmful insofar as it (iii)______ the fields’ mutual enrichment.

Blank 3

答案：D, E　a visionary; a trailblazer

Blank 2
答案：B, D　rampant; a requirement for
rampant

答案：C, E　a prophylactic; an inhibitory

account: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2

China’s rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People’s Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)______ ; nevertheless, the country’s vast size and the isolation of many of its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)______ . This human pressure has (iii)______ the South China tiger.China’s rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People’s Republic. Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive

Researchers have recorded around 60 separate behaviors for worker honeybees, a number that seems to ______ the achievements of many mammals: even the versatile bottlenose dolphin only performs about twice the number a worker honeybee manages.Researchers have recorded around 60 separate behaviors for worker honeybees, a number that seems to ______ the achievements of many mammals: even the versatile bottlenose dolphin only performs about twice the number a worker honeybee manages.
答案：B, E　eclipse; outdo

It is part of (ii)______ in the museum’s architecture and design department, which in the past has championed architecture’s artistic value over its real-world consequences.The museum’s compelling new architectural exhibition looks at eleven projects around the world that have had major (i)______ impacts despite modest budgets. It is part of (ii)______ in the museum’s architecture and design department, which in the past has championed architecture’s artistic value over its real-world consequences.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　social; a shift in philosophy

答案：C, E　polymathic; encyclopedic

#261 Text Completion
Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i)______ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii)______ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i)______ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii)______ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　managing; solving

Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts, because (ii)______ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a science report and would be poorly served by (iii)______.Scientific papers often (i)______ what actually happened in the course of the investigations they describe. Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts, because (ii)______ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a science report and would be poorly served by (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　misrepresent; efficiency; a comprehensive historical account

account: standard unit headword

答案：A, D　slighted; disparaged

commend: standard unit headword

答案：C, D　castigating; chastising

#265 Text Completion
One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)______, exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes. The process is made easier when what we are learning (ii)______ our built-in circuitry. With an understanding of it, we can at least (iii)______ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)______, exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes. The process is made easier when what we are learning (ii)______ our built-in circuitry. With an understanding of it, we can at least (iii)______ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　innate; harmonizes with; adapt

reflect: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　nuanced; complex

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　admirable; unusual

This conclusion would be (ii)______, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)______ of critical thought.Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)______ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)______, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)______ of critical thought.

Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　an impediment to; hasty; stagnation

答案：A, C　senescence; decrepitude

#271 Text Completion
The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforces

rampant: standard unit headword

答案：D　immutable

Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized

Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)______ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)______ .Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)______ . Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating

(i)______ considerations, however, make it difficult to (ii)______ oxytocin levels during sports events: few will willingly interrupt player in the middle of a game in order for scientists to test players’ blood.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　practical; quantify changes in

#305 Text Completion
For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated

But it’s (i)______ to (ii)______ all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it’s just 80 or so—3 percent—that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii)______ kinds of mosquitoes, there’s even one we’d like to see in greater numbers: Taxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. The writer argues that jewelry-grade diamonds, because they are both (i)______ and (ii)______ , prove one of two conclusions; their purchasers have so much money they can spend it on goods that (iii)______ , or their purchasers are so committed to making others think they have such wealth that they are willing to go into debt to do so.The writer argues that jewelry-grade diamonds, because they are both (i)______ and (ii)______ , prove one of two conclusions; their purchasers have so much money they can spend it on goods that (iii)______ , or their purchasers are so committed to making others think they have such wealth that they are willing to go into debt to do so.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　expensive; useless; serve no practical purpose

答案：A　significance

答案：A, D　an idealized; an unflinching

#349 Text Completion
There has been (i)______ elephant’s fabled mental capacities until recently, when these behavioral observations have begun to be (ii)______ by brain science. MRI scans of an elephant’s brain suggest that even relative to its overall size it has a large hippocampus, the component in the mammalian brain linked to memory and an important part of its limbic system, which is involved in processing emotions.There has been (i)______ elephant’s fabled mental capacities until recently, when these behavioral observations have begun to be (ii)______ by brain science. MRI scans of an elephant’s brain suggest that even relative to its overall size it has a large hippocampus, the component in the mammalian brain linked to memory and an important part of its limbic system, which is involved in processing emotions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　only anecdotal evidence for; buttressed

Wolosky’s description (i)______ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)______ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)______ the materialism of her time. 答案：A, D, I　fits; apt; impugned

#356 Text Completion
Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　enterprising; productive; impotence
